Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • CIVIL ENGINEERING • Intermediate • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Pointing to a photograph of a boy Rashid said, “He is the son of the only son of my mother. “How is Rashid related to that boy?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The boy in the photograph is the only son of the son of Rashid’s mother. i.e. the son of Rashid.
Hence, Rashid is the father.

Q10 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • CIVIL ENGINEERING • Intermediate • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A cube is painted red on all faces and it is cut into 125 smaller cubes of equal size.

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
1. Number of cubes which have three faces painted = 8 (because 8 corners)
2. Number of cubes wihich have two faces painted = (5 – 2) × No. of edges = (5 – 2) × 12 = 36
3. Number of cubes which have one face painted = (5 – 2)2 × 6 = 54
4. Number of cubes with no faces painted = (5 – 2)3 × 6 = 27 (Interior cubes)

Consider the following:

Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• M is a Turning Machine that visits state q on some input within 15 steps: Yes we can run all string in language till 15 steps if visit state q on any input then say but can’t say no. [RUN only 15 steps so no loop is formed]. Hence it is decidable.
• Since every Turning Machine can be assigned a unique binary number so with state less then 200 finite Turning Machine is possible, so L2 has finite set of Turning Machine hence it is decidable.

S2 : Every directed acyclic graph has exactly one topological ordering.
Which of the above is true?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

• Some directed acyclic graph can have multiple topological ordering. So, S2 is false.

Q35 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

An undirected graph G on 30 vertices has 4 connected components. What is the minimum number of edges in G?

NAT
Correct answer: 26
Solution

26
A minimal connected graph on n vertices is a tree, with n – 1 edges. If each of the four components is a tree and we join them with edges to form a single component, we would have to add 3 edges. The resulting graph would be a tree with 29 edges on 30 vertices. Hence, the four components originally had 26 edges. This is the same, regardless of how the graph is decomposed.

Not attempted

Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• Listen( ) : Used on server side, causes a bound TCP socket to enter listening state.
• Connect( ) : Initiates a connection on socket.
• Bind( ): It binds a unique local name to the socket with descriptor socket.
• Accept( ): Accepts a received incoming attempt to create new TCP connection from the remote client.

Q53 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) If two graph G1 and G2 are isomorphic, then their complements will always be isomorphic.
(b) If any simple graph with n nodes with n > 1, there are atleast two vertices of same degree.
(c) If two graphs are isomorphic, then they have the same chromatic number.
(d) If two graph G1 and G2 have the same chromatic number, then they are isomorphic.
Which of the above is correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
(d) False: We can give an example of a pair of graphs with the same chromatic number that are not isomorphic.
For example: K3, 3 and K3, 4 both have chromatic number 2 but are not isomorphic.

Correct answer: C

(c)

For the input “((( ))”, the stack does not remain empty after the loop.
So option (c) is true.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) If load factor of hash table is less than 1, then there are no collision.
(b) As the size of hash table increase, the number of collision will decrease.
(c) AVL tree can be used to implemented an optional comparison based sorting algorithm.
(d) Post order traversal is a type of DFS ordering.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
• If multiple elements hashes to same slot then also collision happen even though load is less than 1. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• It is not always the case that, increasing the size of hash table will decrease the number of collision. It actually depends on the search key elements. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• AVL tree can be used to sort N number in Ο(N log N) time by inverting all the member in the tree and iteratively calling next largest N times. So, option (c) is correct.

Q58 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) Deadlock can always be avoided by using semaphore instead of lock for mutual exclusion.
(b) Program counter must be saved when context switch between processes.
(c) Thrashing can always be avoided by swapping.
(d) Each queue have different priority in multilevel queue scheduling.
Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• If you use a semaphore for mutual exclusion, it has all the same properties as a traditional lock.
So, deadlock can’t be avoided always. So, option (a) is false.
• Program counter, stack counter and general purpose register must be saved then context switches. So, option (b) is correct.
• Thrashing can not be avoided by swapping. So, option (c) is false.
• In multilevel feedback queue each queue may use different scheduling algorithm and each queue have different priority. So, option (d) is correct.

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) Delay branch technique is used to optimize the control dependency.
(b) In asynchronous serial transfer of data the two unit share a common clock.
(c) In write through protocol, cache location and main memory location are update simultaneously.
(d) Update bit is used in the write back cache to indicate the cache updation.
Which of the above statement is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• Delay branch rearrange code to reduce control dependency. So, option (a) is correct.
• In synchronous clock is common and in asynchronous clock is different. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Write through protocol update cache and main memory simultaneously. So, option (c) is correct.
• Update bit is used in the write back, cache to indicate the cache updation. So, option (d) is correct.
